# Paints & Coatings Distributor Site — Nigeria-First Research Brief

**Niche ID:** P3-paints-distributor-paints-distributor-site
**Vertical slug:** paints-distributor
**VN-ID:** VN-COM-CON-003
**Niche family:** NF-COM-CON (variant of electrical-fittings)
**Family role:** variant
**Research date:** 2026-04-26

## 1. Nigerian Business Reality
Nigeria's decorative and industrial paint market is valued at over ₦200 billion annually. Key players: Berger Paints Nigeria, Dulux (AkzoNobel), Crown Paints, Jotun, Asian Paints, and locally manufactured brands like Lasgidi Paints. Distribution follows a manufacturer → distributor → retailer model. Distributors serve painters, contractors, facility managers, and DIY customers. Product categories include emulsion (walls), gloss (wood/metal), epoxy floor paint, anti-corrosion (oil tank coating), textured finishes, and varnish. SON certifies local paint manufacturers. WhatsApp colour-shade enquiry and volume pricing is standard.

## 2. Target Audience Profile
- **Interior decorator Chinwe, 35, Port Harcourt**: Sources Dulux or Berger for residential projects. Needs colour matching service, bulk pricing for 20+ litres, delivery to client site.
- **Building contractor Alhaji Sa'id, 50, Kano**: Needs anti-rust primer and gloss for 50-unit estate finishing. Requests volume discount via WhatsApp.
- **Facilities manager Bisi, 42, Lagos**: Sources floor epoxy paint for industrial facility. Needs technical data sheet and SON certification before procurement.

## 3. Recommended Website Structure
- **Home**: Hero (distributor name, "Authorised Berger / Dulux Distributor"), WhatsApp colour enquiry CTA, featured product cards (emulsion, gloss, epoxy, textured with ₦ pricing per litre/bucket), trust strip (SON, brand authorisation, CAC)
- **About**: Business history, brand partnerships, SON compliance, delivery coverage
- **Products**: Decorative paints, industrial coatings, primers, varnishes — all with ₦/litre pricing and colour shade reference
- **Contact**: WhatsApp enquiry (primary), location, volume order form (NDPR-compliant)

## 4. Content Tone & Voice
Professional, colour-creative, and brand-authoritative. "Transform Every Surface", "Authorised Distributor", "Colour Match Service Available". Primary CTA: "Enquire via WhatsApp". Secondary: "Browse Paint Range". Avoid: generic western interior design imagery.

## 5. Trust Signal Inventory
- SON certification for local paint manufacturers
- CAC business registration
- Brand manufacturer authorised distributor certificate (Berger, Dulux, Crown)
- Colour match service availability
- Volume/contractor pricing badge
- Delivery to site notice
- Technical data sheet availability

## 6. Image Direction (5-7 Specific Concepts)
1. Paint display shelves — rows of branded tins (Berger, Dulux), Nigerian distributor warehouse
2. Colour mixing in progress — tinting machine, Nigerian technician, colour swatches
3. Nigerian home interior — freshly painted walls, warm tones, quality finish
4. Industrial floor epoxy — warehouse floor, professional application, Nigerian context
5. Contractor loading paints — bulk purchase, site delivery, Nigerian building site
6. Authorised distributor certificate — framed brand document, trust signal
7. Colour consultation — decorator showing colour chart to Nigerian homeowner

## 7. Regulatory Compliance Checklist
- [ ] SON product certification for paints distributed
- [ ] CAC business registration number
- [ ] Brand manufacturer authorised distributor letter
- [ ] Safety data sheet availability for industrial products
- [ ] NDPR consent on enquiry form

## 8. Nigeria-First Compliance Checklist
- [x] WhatsApp colour enquiry CTA prominent
- [x] SON/brand authorisation trust signals
- [x] Nigerian paint brands referenced (Berger, Dulux, Crown)
- [x] ₦ pricing per litre/bucket/drum
- [x] Nigerian project references (estate, church, office)
- [x] Mobile-first layout
- [x] esc() on all inputs
- [x] NDPR on enquiry form
